query:
These need to be explicitly defined in the test strategy and be included in gate-3b validation requirements: R-01 (High) — EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN gate: This is the correct handling for OQ-03. Can't verify  
  index coverage without a real DB at design time. The deferred delivery gate is appropriate — 
  it's a one-line check that the delivery agent runs before merge. The architecture already    
  argues the UNIQUE constraint covers the NOT EXISTS sub-join (Component 2, SR-04); R-01 is    
  just confirmation. Not a design concern.                                                     

  R-02 (Critical) — "no duplicate" grep gate: This is the most dangerous of the three.         
  test_inserted_edge_is_one_directional was written with explicit intent — it's not an
  accidental one-directional assertion, it's a named contract test. A delivery agent who       
  updates all the numeric edge counts but misses this test will have a passing test suite that
  asserts the wrong behavior. The grep gate must be non-negotiable and should be in the
  delivery brief explicitly, not just the risk strategy.

  R-08 (Critical) — N → 2N edge count blast radius: 8 existing tests + 3 new Group I tests is a
   well-bounded scope. The risk is mechanical — if the delivery agent misses any of the 8, the
  tests silently pass with stale assertions. The risk strategy's concrete list of affected     
  tests (T-BLR-01 through T-BLR-08) gives the delivery agent a checklist, not just a general
  warning. That's the right level of specificity., but otherwise approved to move to delivery

entries.title: Gate 3b: implementation wave delivers production code but zero mandatory tests — entire test modules absent
Test Plan Must Cross-Reference Pseudocode for Edge-Case Behavior Assertions
Circuit breaker / cap logic must be extracted as a testable unit function from the start
validate() implementation requires reading the test plan, not just the pseudocode — boundary tests must ship in the same pass
Non-Negotiable Test Name Verification Pattern for Rewritten Test Suites
